name: Waialua
description_short: Waialua Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving the Waialua, Hawaii community with traditional financial services and NCUA insurance protection.
description_long: Waialua Federal Credit Union is a federally-chartered, not-for-profit credit union operating in Waialua on the island of Oahu, Hawaii. As a federal credit union, it is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and operates under a member-ownership model rather than as a for-profit institution. The credit union has maintained a physical branch presence in the community at 67-292 Goodale Avenue since at least the time this data was compiled. The institution provides standard credit union services typical of community-based financial institutions, including share accounts (savings), share certificates (CDs), and other member financial products. As a federal credit union, deposits are insured up to $250,000 per member account category by the NCUA, providing depositor protection comparable to FDIC insurance at banks. The credit union operates during standard business hours Monday through Friday, serving local members and their financial needs. Waialua Federal Credit Union distinguishes itself as a local, member-focused institution serving a specific geographic community rather than operating as a large regional or national chain. Being a credit union, it operates on a not-for-profit basis, meaning any earnings are theoretically returned to members through better rates or lower fees rather than distributed to shareholders. Its small-town Hawaii location and long-standing community presence suggest local relationship-based banking. No information is available about PALs (Payday Alternative Loans), specific savings rates, loan terms, fees, or comparative advantages. The credit union's actual service offerings and competitive positioning cannot be fully assessed from this location-only data.
services: Member deposit accounts with NCUA insurance
Member financial services (standard credit union offerings)
In-person branch banking at Waialua location
pros: NCUA-insured deposits up to $250,000 per account category for member protection
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means earnings benefit members rather than shareholders
Established local credit union with physical branch in Waialua community
Accessible phone contact (808-637-6737) for member inquiries
Consistent weekday operating hours (8:30 AM - 4:00 PM Monday-Friday)
Federal credit union regulation provides oversight and compliance standards
cons: Website contains only basic location/hours information—no details on rates, fees, or specific products
Limited to weekday hours only with no Saturday/Sunday availability for busy members
No information on membership eligibility criteria or requirements
Single branch location limits convenience for members outside immediate Waialua area
Unknown service offerings beyond standard credit union products—no loan or investment details provided
best_for: Waialua, Hawaii residents seeking local community banking with member-ownership benefits
Individuals wanting NCUA-insured savings and checking accounts in the Oahu area
Members prioritizing not-for-profit financial institutions over for-profit banks
diagnosis: Waialua Federal Credit Union is best for Hawaii residents in the Waialua area seeking community-based, member-owned banking with NCUA insurance protection. The primary caveat is that publicly available information is extremely limited to basic branch details; prospective members must contact the credit union directly for information on membership eligibility, specific loan products, rates, fees, and full service offerings.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
